title: Catamaran Hire at Sunset in Fuengirola: 2026 Costs
html:
Catamaran hire at sunset in Fuengirola means casting off from Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola as the light softens over the Costa del Sol, with most evening sailings on BoatHire24 priced from around EUR 350 to EUR 700 for the boat. The calm Alboran Sea makes the golden hour genuinely easy.

Where to set off in Fuengirola

Most sunset catamaran trips leave from Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, the town marina just below the Sohail Castle, and from there the coast opens both ways.

  • Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola: the main marina and the usual departure point for evening sailings.
  • Castillo Sohail: the headland and beach beside the marina, a natural backdrop as you motor out.
  • Carvajal and Torreblanca: quieter stretches of coast just east, good for a calm anchored swim.
  • Benalmadena and Puerto Marina: a short hop north along the bay, often included on longer cruises.
  • Mijas Costa and La Cala de Mijas: the low cliffs west of town, scenic in the late sun.

Which catamaran to rent in Fuengirola

Sailing catamarans suit Fuengirola well because the twin hulls stay flat and stable when half your group is standing with a drink at sunset. Most carry 10 to 30 guests.

  1. Sailing catamaran (40 to 43 ft): best for families and small groups of up to 12, with shaded seating and a swim ladder, from EUR 350.
  2. Leopard catamaran (50 to 53 ft): a roomier deck for 15 to 25 guests on a celebration, from EUR 550. The Leopard 40 ft known as this catamaran is a typical sailing example for the bay.
  3. Power catamaran: faster between coves and good for covering more coastline, from EUR 600.
  4. Large event catamaran (60 ft plus): built for parties of 25 to 40, with a wide bow and sound system, from EUR 900.
  5. Motor cruiser: an alternative for couples who want a quick private sunset lap, from EUR 300.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran in Fuengirola at sunset?

Expect roughly EUR 30 to EUR 50 per person on a shared sailing, or EUR 350 to EUR 900 for a private catamaran depending on size and length. The bands below give a clearer picture for 2026, and you can rent a boat with a skipper included so nobody in your party needs a licence.

  • A shared sunset tour ticket usually costs EUR 30 to EUR 50 per person in Fuengirola.
  • A small private motorboat for a half-day starts near EUR 250 in EUR.
  • A private catamaran for a full day typically runs EUR 700 to EUR 1,200 in EUR.
  • A crewed yacht with skipper and hostess generally starts around EUR 1,500 per day in EUR.

When is the best season, and do I need a licence?

High season runs June to September, with July and August the warmest and busiest, so book your sunset slot a week or two ahead. The coast is mild from May into October, and the afternoon levante, an easterly wind, can build the swell, while the westerly poniente often brings flatter evenings. Spain follows the principle that a skippered charter needs no licence from the guest, because the qualified skipper holds the certification; for any self-drive option the operator confirms exact requirements. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Fuengirola or list your own.

Aim to board around an hour before sunset in Fuengirola: in midsummer the sun sets close to 21:30 local time, so an evening sailing lands you in calm water with the light at its best and the day-trip boats already back in port.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 350 to EUR 900 for a private sunset catamaran.
  • Peak months: June, July, August and September.
  • Trip length: two to three hours for a sunset sailing.
  • Capacity: typically 10 to 30 guests per catamaran.
  • Departure: Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola.
  • Currency: EUR.

Questions about boat rental in Fuengirola

How far does a sunset catamaran sail from Fuengirola?

A standard evening sailing covers only a few nautical miles, usually two to four along the bay toward Benalmadena or Mijas Costa, since the aim is calm anchored time rather than distance within the two to three hour window.

Can children come on a sunset catamaran?

Children are welcome on almost every catamaran in Fuengirola, and the flat twin-hull deck suits families well, with the skipper providing life jackets in suitable sizes; do confirm the youngest guest's age with the operator when you book.

What should we bring for a sunset trip?

Bring a light layer for when the sun drops, flat shoes, sun cream and a swimsuit, as the water off Fuengirola stays swimmable well into the evening through summer; most boats supply towels and a cool box on request.

Is a skipper always included?

For sunset catamaran hire in Fuengirola a skipper is almost always included in the price, which is why no guest needs a licence; bareboat self-drive is offered only on smaller motorboats with the right paperwork.

An evening on the Alboran Sea is one of the easiest ways to mark a birthday or anniversary, and the calm bay does most of the work. To compare crews, decks and prices for your date, browse the listings and message owners directly through BoatHire24.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Fuengirola and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
